other_observers: none with me; not sure who found the bird
description: the bird was a fairly large gull, about the same size or slightly larger than nearby Herring Gulls and much larger than nearby Ring-billed Gulls. The bill was relatively large for a gull, and noticeably "blob-tipped". It was black, with a shadow of pinkish color at the base. The legs were pink, about the same shade as nearby Herring Gulls.  The entire plumage of the bird was a pale brown, paler and much more uniform than the brown of Herring Gull. The bird had a faintly darker helmet of color on the head, contrasting with the slightly paler color of the nape and neck. The wingtips were the same color as the body, and were both short and blunt. The tail was slightly but noticeably darker.  The overall shape of the bird appeared bulky, accentuated by a relatively thick neck, short and broad wings, and large and blob-tipped bill.Behaviors: the bird was seen loafing around on the ice-edge for the most part, but also actively feeding on the water and on the iceCall: none heardPlumage: 1st cycle
photographer: Photographer: Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer
